description:
## Finding

When `tokio::task::spawn_blocking` returns a `JoinError` (task panicked or was cancelled), the two hardware-backed signing crates handle it differently:

**secretx-pkcs11** extracts a reusable helper:
```rust
fn map_join_err(e: tokio::task::JoinError) -> SecretError {
    pkcs11_err(format!("task join error: {e}"))
}
// Used at 4 call sites: .await.map_err(map_join_err)?
```

**secretx-tpm2** inlines the same logic 4 times:
```rust
.await
.map_err(|e| SecretError::Backend {
    backend: BACKEND,
    source: format!("TPM task panicked: {e}").into(),
})?
```

## Impact

- **Maintenance**: if the error classification for JoinError changes (e.g. to `Unavailable` for task cancellation vs `Backend` for panics), tpm2 requires editing 4 identical closures.
- **Consistency risk for future crates**: a new sibling crate author copying from tpm2 will duplicate the inline pattern; copying from pkcs11 will use the helper. The workspace has no convention.
- The `"TPM task panicked"` message is also imprecise — `JoinError` can represent cancellation, not just panics.

## Locations

- `/home/mark/PROJECT/crate-secretx/secretx-tpm2/src/lib.rs` lines 299–303, 353–357, 421–425, 467–471
- `/home/mark/PROJECT/crate-secretx/secretx-pkcs11/src/lib.rs` lines 441–444
title: JoinError mapping: inline duplication in TPM2 vs extracted helper in PKCS11
